verses.text: The LORD: I have made you as God to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet. You shall speak all that I command, and Aaron shall speak to Pharaoh that he send the children of Israel out of his land. Guidance: God answers Moses' I-am-of-uncircumcised-lips by reframing the work. Don't argue with how God assigns the team; just take your part.

The LORD: I have made you as God to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet. You shall speak all that I command, and Aaron shall speak to Pharaoh that he send the children of Israel out of his land. Guidance: God answers Moses' I-am-of-uncircumcised-lips by reframing the work. Don't argue with how God assigns the team; just take your part.

I will harden Pharaoh's heart and multiply My signs and wonders in Egypt. Pharaoh will not listen. I will lay My hand upon Egypt and bring out My people by great judgments. The Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD. Guidance: Even resistance has a purpose in God's plan. The point is not only the freedom of Israel, but the knowing of God by Egypt.

I will harden Pharaoh's heart and multiply My signs and wonders in Egypt. Pharaoh will not listen. I will lay My hand upon Egypt and bring out My people by great judgments. The Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD. Guidance: Even resistance has a purpose in God's plan. The point is not only the freedom of Israel, but the knowing of God by Egypt.

I will harden Pharaoh's heart and multiply My signs and wonders in Egypt. Pharaoh will not listen. I will lay My hand upon Egypt and bring out My people by great judgments. The Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD. Guidance: Even resistance has a purpose in God's plan. The point is not only the freedom of Israel, but the knowing of God by Egypt.

Moses and Aaron did as the LORD commanded them — so did they. Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty-three, when they spoke to Pharaoh. Guidance: The text underlines they obeyed simply. Old age does not exempt anyone from a fresh assignment. God uses your final decades as much as your first.

Moses and Aaron did as the LORD commanded them — so did they. Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty-three, when they spoke to Pharaoh. Guidance: The text underlines they obeyed simply. Old age does not exempt anyone from a fresh assignment. God uses your final decades as much as your first.

The LORD: when Pharaoh asks, show me a miracle, then say to Aaron — take your rod and cast it before Pharaoh; it shall become a serpent. Guidance: God anticipates the questions you will be asked and equips you for them in advance. The miracle Moses needed was waiting in the staff he had already been holding.

The LORD: when Pharaoh asks, show me a miracle, then say to Aaron — take your rod and cast it before Pharaoh; it shall become a serpent. Guidance: God anticipates the questions you will be asked and equips you for them in advance. The miracle Moses needed was waiting in the staff he had already been holding.

Moses and Aaron go in to Pharaoh and do as the LORD commanded. Aaron casts down his rod, and it becomes a serpent. Guidance: The first sign is delivered exactly as instructed. Obedience does not always require boldness — sometimes just doing the small thing as commanded.

Pharaoh calls his wise men and sorcerers. The magicians of Egypt match the trick — they too cast down rods that become serpents. But Aaron's rod swallows up their rods. Guidance: The enemy can imitate God's power up to a point. The decisive sign is not that miracles happen — but who consumes whom.

Pharaoh calls his wise men and sorcerers. The magicians of Egypt match the trick — they too cast down rods that become serpents. But Aaron's rod swallows up their rods. Guidance: The enemy can imitate God's power up to a point. The decisive sign is not that miracles happen — but who consumes whom.

Pharaoh's heart hardens; he does not listen to them, as the LORD had said. Guidance: A sign by itself does not soften a hard heart. Some people will see miracles and still refuse. The witness is recorded anyway.

The LORD: Pharaoh's heart is hardened; he refuses to let the people go. Go to him in the morning, when he goes out to the water; stand by the river's brink with the rod in your hand. Guidance: God meets confrontation with strategy. Sometimes obedience requires being in the right place at the right hour, with what He has placed in your hand.

The LORD: Pharaoh's heart is hardened; he refuses to let the people go. Go to him in the morning, when he goes out to the water; stand by the river's brink with the rod in your hand. Guidance: God meets confrontation with strategy. Sometimes obedience requires being in the right place at the right hour, with what He has placed in your hand.

Tell him: the LORD God of the Hebrews has sent me — let My people go. So far you would not hear. Now you will know I am the LORD: I will strike the river with the rod, and it will turn to blood. The fish will die; the river will stink; the Egyptians will loathe their water. Guidance: God targets the very thing Egypt depended on. Idols turn into agonies.

Tell him: the LORD God of the Hebrews has sent me — let My people go. So far you would not hear. Now you will know I am the LORD: I will strike the river with the rod, and it will turn to blood. The fish will die; the river will stink; the Egyptians will loathe their water. Guidance: God targets the very thing Egypt depended on. Idols turn into agonies.

Tell him: the LORD God of the Hebrews has sent me — let My people go. So far you would not hear. Now you will know I am the LORD: I will strike the river with the rod, and it will turn to blood. The fish will die; the river will stink; the Egyptians will loathe their water. Guidance: God targets the very thing Egypt depended on. Idols turn into agonies.

The LORD tells Aaron: stretch your hand over the waters of Egypt — every river, pond, vessel — let them become blood. Moses and Aaron obey; the river turns to blood before Pharaoh and his servants. Fish die; the water cannot be drunk; blood is throughout all Egypt. Guidance: The Nile that swallowed Hebrew babies now bleeds. God's judgments often answer specific historic wounds, not random ones.

Fish die; the water cannot be drunk; blood is throughout all Egypt. Guidance: The Nile that swallowed Hebrew babies now bleeds. God's judgments often answer specific historic wounds, not random ones.

The LORD tells Aaron: stretch your hand over the waters of Egypt — every river, pond, vessel — let them become blood. Moses and Aaron obey; the river turns to blood before Pharaoh and his servants. Fish die; the water cannot be drunk; blood is throughout all Egypt. Guidance: The Nile that swallowed Hebrew babies now bleeds. God's judgments often answer specific historic wounds, not random ones.

The magicians replicate the sign with their secret arts; so Pharaoh's heart is hardened. He does not listen. He turns and goes into his house and does not take this to heart. Guidance: Even those who can imitate God's signs cannot reverse them. Pharaoh's choice to ignore is itself an act — note when our shrugging is a decision.

The magicians replicate the sign with their secret arts; so Pharaoh's heart is hardened. He does not listen. He turns and goes into his house and does not take this to heart. Guidance: Even those who can imitate God's signs cannot reverse them. Pharaoh's choice to ignore is itself an act — note when our shrugging is a decision.

All the Egyptians dig around the river for water, because they cannot drink from it. Seven days pass after the LORD smote the river. Guidance: Judgment leaves people scrambling for what they used to take for granted. Seven days is enough time to feel the cost — and not yet long enough to soften the heart.
